what_it_does: Acceptance test suite that verifies skill:// and context:// URI schemes are registered in contracts.address.SCHEMES, that SkillRegistry and ContextRegistry discover filesystem entries like RoleRegistry, that resolve_address dispatches to those registries and returns real declared content, that unknown ids raise fail-loud registry-is-truth errors, that still-unbuilt schemes continue to raise, and that a fireable role run over a skill:// unit receives the skill's instructions as primary input; it also checks AGENTS.md drift homes.
observations: Uses a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper for all assertions.
Dynamically writes and removes probe and malformed files under skills/ to test discovery, registration, unregistration, and fail-loud validation.
Probes for a live resident 4B model and falls back to mocking fabric.client.complete when the model is unavailable.
Compares resolve_address output directly to SkillRegistry.read and ContextRegistry.read to prove a single resolution seam.
Tests end-to-end run_items with a skill:// unit both live and mocked, capturing the role's input to show the skill content is consumed.
Verifies that AGENTS.md drift homes list every discovered skill/context id and name the registries.
References a C 3b / C 3/4 / C9.2 taxonomy in its module docstring as the motivating design context.
standouts: Dynamic drop-in proof: writing a new skills/<id>.py file and asserting it is discovered without any code change, then removing it and asserting rediscovery unregisters it.
Live-or-mocked end-to-end: actually attempts a real model completion, and if unavailable stubs the client to prove the skill address reaches the role input.
Fail-loud adversarial tests for malformed skill entries (id mismatch, empty content, unknown field) and unknown skill/context ids.
Reuse proof: resolve_address('skill://...') and SkillRegistry().discover(...).read(...) return identical content.
conventions: Follows the acceptance-test pattern with a check helper and a running PASS counter.
Mirrors the structure of roles_acceptance tests: discover, rediscover, register, read, drift checks.
Uses filesystem discovery from skills/ and contexts/ directories rather than hard-coded id lists.
Breaks strict test isolation by mutating files in the real skills/ directory during execution, relying on try/finally cleanup.
Monkey-patches cognition.client.complete in the mocked branch.
concerns: Tests mutate the real skills/ directory; concurrent runs or interrupted cleanup could leave probe or malformed files behind.
Relies on substring matches in seed skill/context content ('summarizing', 'company', 'load-bearing') that may drift.
The live model probe consumes a real completion call when the resident model is up.
Hard-codes the 'focus' role and assumes it is fireable and utterance-only.
The still-unbuilt scheme test uses a malformed-looking vec://run://x#emb=m URI but only checks the error message substring.
notes: Acceptance test proving skill and context registries are first-class addressable resources and that a role can consume a skill:// address as its primary input.
questions: What is the exact shape of SkillRegistry, ContextRegistry, and Entry classes?
What do the seed skill and context files actually contain beyond the checked substrings?
How does contracts.address.SCHEMES and scheme() manage registered vs unbuilt schemes?
How does run_items classify units and route a skill:// address through resolve_address?
What is the resident 4B model and how is cognition.RESIDENT_BASE_URL configured?
What do the 'C 3b', 'C 3/4', and 'C9.2' labels refer to in the project taxonomy?
How are malformed skill entries defined in SkillRegistry validation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is an acceptance test for skills/contexts as addressable registries, scheme registration, resolve_address dispatch, fail-loud behavior, role-reads-a-skill end-to-end, and drift documentation.
intention_signals: Extend the URI resolution seam so skills and contexts become discoverable, addressable resources that can be fed directly into roles, replacing hard-coded lists with filesystem registries and keeping the system honest through fail-loud validation.
novelty: First real extension of the resolve_address seam beyond run/cas/blob/ui/code to skill:// and context://, with an acceptance test that dynamically proves drop-in registration and live-or-mocked end-to-end role consumption of a skill address.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that encodes the contract for skill/context registries, scheme registration, resolve_address dispatch, fail-loud unknown ids, the role-reads-a-skill use case, and drift checks tying seed entries to AGENTS.md.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test suite verifying skill:// and context:// schemes, SkillRegistry/ContextRegistry discovery, resolve_address dispatch, fail-loud behavior, and end-to-end role consumption of skill addresses with drift checks.
intention_for_embedding: Validate the scope expansion making skills and contexts addressable registries integrated into the resolve_address seam and usable as role inputs.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: The central check(label, cond) function used throughout the suite; it asserts cond, increments the global PASS counter, and prints 'ok <label>' for every passing assertion.

symbols.does: Probes whether the resident 4B model is reachable.
symbols.description: Opens an OpenAI transport via fabric and attempts a tiny completion; if it succeeds the suite runs the end-to-end test live, otherwise it falls back to a mocked client.

symbols.does: Minimal wrapper that mimics a Pydantic model response.
symbols.description: A small helper class that stores a dict and exposes model_dump() so the mocked complete() return value looks like a structured model response.
Initializes the wrapper with the given dict d, storing it in self._d for later retrieval by model_dump().

symbols.does: Returns a copy of the wrapped dict.
symbols.description: Imitates Pydantic's model_dump by returning a shallow copy of the wrapped dict, used by the mocked client to produce a serializable response.

symbols.does: Mock implementation of fabric.client.complete that captures role input.
symbols.description: Stand-in for the real completion client when the resident model is unavailable; it records the last user message content into seen_users and returns a fixed _Validated response so the suite can verify the skill content reached the role.
